java.util.Collection;\\n77: \\n78: import static org.apache.flink.util.Preconditions.checkArgument;\\n79: import static org.apache.flink.util.Preconditions.checkNotNull;\\n80: \\n81: /**\\n82:  * An operator that implements the logic for windowing based on a {@link WindowAssigner} and {@link\\n83:  * Trigger}.\\n84:  *\\n85:  * <p>When an element arrives it gets assigned a key using a {@link KeySelector} and it gets\\n86:  * assigned to zero or more windows using a {@link WindowAssigner}. Based on this, the element is\\n87:  * put into panes. A pane is the bucket of elements that have the same key and same {@code Window}.\\n88:  * An element can be in multiple panes if it was assigned to multiple windows by the {@code\\n89:  * WindowAssigner}.\\n90:  *\\n91:  * <p>Each pane gets its own instance of the provided {@code Trigger}. This trigger determines when\\n92:  * the contents of the pane should be processed to emit results. When a trigger fires, the given\\n93:  * {@link InternalWindowFunction} is invoked to produce the results that are emitted for the pane to\\n94:  * which the {@code Trigger} belongs.\\n95:  *\\n96:  * @param <K> The type of key returned by the {@code KeySelector}.\\n97:  * @param <IN> The type of the incoming elements.\\n98:  * @param <OUT> The type of elements emitted by the {@code InternalWindowFunction}.\\n99:  * @param <W> The type of {@code Window} that the {@code WindowAssigner} assigns.\\n100:  */\\n101: @Internal\\n102: public class WindowOperator<K, IN, ACC, OUT, W extends Window>\\n103:         extends AbstractUdfStreamOperator<OUT, InternalWindowFunction<ACC, OUT, K, W>>\\n104:         implements OneInputStreamOperator<IN, OUT>, Triggerable<K, W> {\\n105: \\n106:     private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\\n107: \\n108:     // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n109:     // Configuration values and user functions\\n110:     // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n111: \\n112:     protected final WindowAssigner<? super IN, W> windowAssigner;\\n113: \\n114:     private final KeySelector<IN, K> keySelector;\\n115: \\n116:     private final Trigger<? super IN, ? super W> trigger;\\n117: \\n118:     private final StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, ACC>, ?> windowStateDescriptor;\\n119: \\n120:     /** For serializing the key in checkpoints. */\\n121:     protected final TypeSerializer<K> keySerializer;\\n122: \\n123:     /** For serializing the window in checkpoints. */\\n124:     protected final TypeSerializer<W> windowSerializer;\\n125: \\n126:     /**\\n127:      * The allowed lateness for elements. This is used for:\\n128:      *\\n129:      * <ul>\\n130:      *   <li>Deciding if an element should be dropped from a window due to lateness.\\n131:      *   <li>Clearing the state of a window if the system time passes the {@code window.maxTimestamp\\n132:      *       + allowedLateness} landmark.\\n133:      * </ul>\\n134:      */\\n135:     protected final long allowedLateness;\\n136: \\n137:     /**\\n138:      * {@link OutputTag} to use for late arriving events. Elements for which {@code\\n139:      * window.maxTimestamp + allowedLateness} is smaller than the current watermark will be emitted\\n140:      * to this.\\n141:      */\\n142:     protected final OutputTag<IN> lateDataOutputTag;\\n143: \\n144:     private static final String LATE_ELEMENTS_DROPPED_METRIC_NAME = \\\"numLateRecordsDropped\\\";\\n145: \\n146:     protected transient Counter numLateRecordsDropped;\\n147: \\n148:     // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n149:     // State that is not checkpointed\\n150:     // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n151: \\n152:     /** The state in which the window contents is stored. java.util.Collection;\\n77: \\n78: import static org.apache.flink.util.Preconditions.checkArgument;\\n79: import static org.apache.flink.util.Preconditions.checkNotNull;\\n80: \\n81: /**\\n82:  * An operator that implements the logic for windowing based on a {@link WindowAssigner} and {@link\\n83:  * Trigger}.\\n84:  *\\n85:  * <p>When an element arrives it gets assigned a key using a {@link KeySelector} and it gets\\n86:  * assigned to zero or more windows using a {@link WindowAssigner}. Based on this, the element is\\n87:  * put into panes. A pane is the bucket of elements that have the same key and same {@code Window}.\\n88:  * An element can be in multiple panes if it was assigned to multiple windows by the {@code\\n89:  * WindowAssigner}.\\n90:  *\\n91:  * <p>Each pane gets its own instance of the provided {@code Trigger}. This trigger determines when\\n92:  * the contents of the pane should be processed to emit results. When a trigger fires, the given\\n93:  * {@link InternalWindowFunction} is invoked to produce the results that are emitted for the pane to\\n94:  * which the {@code Trigger} belongs.\\n95:  *\\n96:  * @param <K> The type of key returned by the {@code KeySelector}.\\n97:  * @param <IN> The type of the incoming elements.\\n98:  * @param <OUT> The type of elements emitted by the {@code InternalWindowFunction}.\\n99:  * @param <W> The type of {@code Window} that the {@code WindowAssigner} assigns.\\n100:  */\\n101: @Internal\\n102: public class WindowOperator<K, IN, ACC, OUT, W extends Window>\\n103:         extends AbstractUdfStreamOperator<OUT, InternalWindowFunction<ACC, OUT, K, W>>\\n104:         implements OneInputStreamOperator<IN, OUT>, Triggerable<K, W> {\\n105: \\n106:     private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\\n107: \\n108:     // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n109:     // Configuration values and user functions\\n110:     // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n111: \\n112:     protected final WindowAssigner<? super IN, W> windowAssigner;\\n113: \\n114:     private final KeySelector<IN, K> keySelector;\\n115: \\n116:     private final Trigger<? super IN, ? super W> trigger;\\n117: \\n118:     private final StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, ACC>, ?> windowStateDescriptor;\\n119: \\n120:     /** For serializing the key in checkpoints. */\\n121:     protected final TypeSerializer<K> keySerializer;\\n122: \\n123:     /** For serializing the window in checkpoints. */\\n124:     protected final TypeSerializer<W> windowSerializer;\\n125: \\n126:     /**\\n127:      * The allowed lateness for elements.     }\\n349: \\n350:                                         triggerContext.key = key;\\n351:                                         triggerContext.window = mergeResult;\\n352: \\n353:                                         triggerContext.onMerge(mergedWindows);\\n354: \\n355:                                         for (W m : mergedWindows) {\\n356:      } else {\\n444:                 this.numLateRecordsDropped.inc();\\n445:             }\\n446:         }\\n447:     }\\n448: \\n449:     @Override\\n450:     public void onEventTime(InternalTimer<K, W> timer) throws Exception {\\n451:         triggerContext.key = timer.getKey();\\n452:         triggerContext.window = timer.getNamespace();\\n453: \\n454: java.util.Collection;\n77: \n78: import static org.apache.flink.util.Preconditions.checkArgument;\n79: import static org.apache.flink.util.Preconditions.checkNotNull;\n80: \n81: /**\n82:  * An operator that implements the logic for windowing based on a {@link WindowAssigner} and {@link\n83:  * Trigger}.\n84:  *\n85:  * <p>When an element arrives it gets assigned a key using a {@link KeySelector} and it gets\n86:  * assigned to zero or more windows using a {@link WindowAssigner}. Based on this, the element is\n87:  * put into panes. A pane is the bucket of elements that have the same key and same {@code Window}.\n88:  * An element can be in multiple panes if it was assigned to multiple windows by the {@code\n89:  * WindowAssigner}.\n90:  *\n91:  * <p>Each pane gets its own instance of the provided {@code Trigger}. This trigger determines when\n92:  * the contents of the pane should be processed to emit results. When a trigger fires, the given\n93:  * {@link InternalWindowFunction} is invoked to produce the results that are emitted for the pane to\n94:  * which the {@code Trigger} belongs.\n95:  *\n96:  * @param <K> The type of key returned by the {@code KeySelector}.\n97:  * @param <IN> The type of the incoming elements.\n98:  * @param <OUT> The type of elements emitted by the {@code InternalWindowFunction}.\n99:  * @param <W> The type of {@code Window} that the {@code WindowAssigner} assigns.\n100:  */\n101: @Internal\n102: public class WindowOperator<K, IN, ACC, OUT, W extends Window>\n103:         extends AbstractUdfStreamOperator<OUT, InternalWindowFunction<ACC, OUT, K, W>>\n104:         implements OneInputStreamOperator<IN, OUT>, Triggerable<K, W> {\n105: \n106:     private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n107: \n108:     // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n109:     // Configuration values and user functions\n110:     // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n111: \n112:     protected final WindowAssigner<? super IN, W> windowAssigner;\n113: \n114:     private final KeySelector<IN, K> keySelector;\n115: \n116:     private final Trigger<? super IN, ? super W> trigger;\n117: \n118:     private final StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, ACC>, ?> windowStateDescriptor;\n119: \n120:     /** For serializing the key in checkpoints. */\n121:     protected final TypeSerializer<K> keySerializer;\n122: \n123:     /** For serializing the window in checkpoints. */\n124:     protected final TypeSerializer<W> windowSerializer;\n125: \n126:     /**\n127:      * The allowed lateness for elements. This is used for:\n128:      *\n129:      * <ul>\n130:      *   <li>Deciding if an element should be dropped from a window due to lateness.\n131:      *   <li>Clearing the state of a window if the system time passes the {@code window.maxTimestamp\n132:      *       + allowedLateness} landmark.\n133:      * </ul>\n134:      */\n135:     protected final long allowedLateness;\n136: \n137:     /**\n138:      * {@link OutputTag} to use for late arriving events. Elements for which {@code\n139:      * window.maxTimestamp + allowedLateness} is smaller than the current watermark will be emitted\n140:      * to this.\n141:      */\n142:     protected final OutputTag<IN> lateDataOutputTag;\n143: \n144:     private static final String LATE_ELEMENTS_DROPPED_METRIC_NAME = \"numLateRecordsDropped\";\n145: \n146:     protected transient Counter numLateRecordsDropped;\n147: \n148:     // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n149:     // State that is not checkpointed\n150:     // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n151: \n152:     /** The state in which the window contents is stored. Each window is a namespace */\n153:     private transient InternalAppendingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC> windowState;\n154: \n155:     /**\n156:      * The {@link #windowState}, typed to merging state for merging windows. Null if the window\n157:      * state is not mergeable.\n158:      */\n159:     private transient InternalMergingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC> windowMergingState;\n160: \n161:     /** The state that holds the merging window metadata (the sets that describe what is merged). */\n162:     private transient InternalListState<K, VoidNamespace, Tuple2<W, W>> mergingSetsState;\n163: \n164:     /**\n165: protected transient WindowAssigner.WindowAssignerContext windowAssignerContext;\n175: \n176:     // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n177:     // State that needs to be checkpointed\n178:     // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n179: \n180:     protected transient InternalTimerService<W> internalTimerService;\n181: \n182:     /** Creates a new {@code WindowOperator} based on the given policies and user functions. */\n183:     public WindowOperator(\n184:             WindowAssigner<? super IN, W> windowAssigner,\n185:             TypeSerializer<W> windowSerializer,\n186:             KeySelector<IN, K> keySelector,\n187:             TypeSerializer<K> keySerializer,\n188:             StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, ACC>, ?> windowStateDescriptor,\n189:             InternalWindowFunction<ACC, OUT, K, W> windowFunction,\n190:             Trigger<? super IN, ? super W> trigger,\n191:             long allowedLateness,\n192:             OutputTag<IN> lateDataOutputTag) {\n193: \n194:         super(windowFunction);\n195: \n196:         checkArgument(allowedLateness >= 0);\n197: \n198:         checkArgument(\n199:                 windowStateDescriptor == null || windowStateDescriptor.isSerializerInitialized(),\n200:                 \"window state serializer is not properly initialized\");\n201: \n202:         this.windowAssigner = checkNotNull(windowAssigner);\n203:         this.windowSerializer = checkNotNull(windowSerializer);\n204:         this.keySelector = this.numLateRecordsDropped = metrics.counter(LATE_ELEMENTS_DROPPED_METRIC_NAME);\n230:         timestampedCollector = new TimestampedCollector<>(output);\n231: \n232:         internalTimerService =        windowAssignerContext =\n238:                 new WindowAssigner.WindowAssignerContext() {\n239:                     @Override\n240:                     public long getCurrentProcessingTime() {\n241:                         return internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\n242:                     }\n243:                 };\n244: \n245:         // create (or restore) the state that }\n252: \n253:         // create the typed and helper states for merging windows\n254:         if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n255: \n256:             // store a typed reference for the state of merging windows - sanity check\n257:             if (windowState instanceof InternalMergingState) {\n258:                 windowMergingState = (InternalMergingState<K, W, assigner, but the window state is not mergeable.\");\n262:             }\n263: \n264:             @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n265:             final Class<Tuple2<W, W>> typedTuple =         typedTuple, new TypeSerializer[] {windowSerializer, windowSerializer});\n270: \n271:             final ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<W, W>> mergingSetsStateDescriptor =\n272: Exception {\n285:         super.close();\n286:         timestampedCollector = null;\n287:         triggerContext = null;\n288:         processContext = null;\n289:         windowAssignerContext = null;\n290:     }\n291: \n292:     @Override\n293:     public void processElement(StreamRecord<IN> element) throws Exception {\n294:         final Collection<W> elementWindows =\n295:                 windowAssigner.assignWindows(\n296:                         element.getValue(), element.getTimestamp(), windowAssignerContext);\n297: \n298:         // if element is handled by none of assigned elementWindows\n299:         boolean isSkippedElement = true;\n300: \n301:         final K key = this.<K>getKeyedStateBackend().getCurrentKey();\n302: \n303:         if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n304:             MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n305: \n306:             for (W window : elementWindows) {\n307: \n308:                 // adding the new window might result in a merge, in that case the actualWindow\n309:                 // is the merged window and we work with that. If we don't merge then\n310:                 // actualWindow == window\n311:                 W actualWindow =\n312:                         mergingWindows.addWindow(\n313:                                 window,\n314:                                 new MergingWindowSet.MergeFunction<W>() {\n315:                                     @Override\n316:                                     public void merge(\n317:                                             W mergeResult,\n318:                                             Collection<W> mergedWindows,\n319:                                             W stateWindowResult,\n320:                                             Collection<W> mergedStateWindows)\n321:                                             throws Exception {\n322: \n323:                                         if ((windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n324:                                                 && mergeResult.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness\n325:                                                         <= internalTimerService\n326:                                                                 .currentWatermark())) {\n327:                                             throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n328:                                                     \"The end timestamp of an \"\n329:                                                             + \"event-time window cannot become earlier than the current watermark \"\n330:                                                             + \"by merging. Current watermark: \"\n331:                                                             + internalTimerService\n332:                                                                     .currentWatermark()\n333:                                                             + \" window: \"\n334:                                                             + mergeResult);\n335:                                         } else if (!windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n336:                                             long currentProcessingTime =\n337:                                                     internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\n338:                                             if (mergeResult.maxTimestamp()\n339:                                                     <= currentProcessingTime) {\n340:                                                 throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n341:                                                         \"The end timestamp of a \"\n342:                                                                 + \"processing-time window cannot become earlier than the current processing time \"\n343:                                                                 + \"by merging. Current processing time: \"\n344:                                                                 + currentProcessingTime\n345:                                                                 + \" window: \"\n346:                                                                 + mergeResult);\n347:                                             }\n348:                                         }\n349: \n350:                                         triggerContext.key = key;\n351:                                         triggerContext.window = mergeResult;\n352: \n353:                                         triggerContext.onMerge(mergedWindows);\n354: \n355:                                         for (W m : mergedWindows) {\n356:                                             triggerContext.window = m;\n357:                                             triggerContext.clear();\n358:                                             deleteCleanupTimer(m);\n359:                                         }\n360: \n361:                                         // merge the merged state windows into the newly resulting\n362:                                         // state window\n363:                                         windowMergingState.mergeNamespaces(\n364:                                                 stateWindowResult, mergedStateWindows);\n365:                                     }\n366:                                 });\n367: \n368:                 // drop if the window is already late\n369:                 if (isWindowLate(actualWindow)) {\n370:                     mergingWindows.retireWindow(actualWindow);\n371:                     continue;\n372:                 }\n373:                 isSkippedElement = false;\n374: \n375:                 W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(actualWindow);\n376:                 if (stateWindow == null) {\n377:                     throw new IllegalStateException(\n378:                             \"Window \" + window + \" is not in in-flight window set.\");\n379:                 }\n380: \n381:                 windowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n382:                 windowState.add(element.getValue());\n383: \n384:                 triggerContext.key = key;\n385:                 triggerContext.window = actualWindow;\n386: \n387:                 TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onElement(element);\n388: \n389:                 if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n390:                     ACC contents = windowState.get();\n391:                     if (contents != null) {\n392:                         emitWindowContents(actualWindow, contents);\n393:                     }\n394:                 }\n395: \n396:                 if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n397:                     windowState.clear();\n398:                 }\n399:                 registerCleanupTimer(actualWindow);\n400:             }\n401: \n402:             // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n403:             mergingWindows.persist();\n404:         } else {\n405:             for (W window : elementWindows) {\n406: \n407:                 // drop if the window is already late\n408:                 if (isWindowLate(window)) {\n409:                     continue;\n410:                 }\n411:                 isSkippedElement = false;\n412: \n413:                 windowState.setCurrentNamespace(window);\n414:                 windowState.add(element.getValue());\n415: \n416:                 triggerContext.key = key;\n417:                 triggerContext.window = window;\n418: \n419:                 TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onElement(element);\n420: \n421:                 if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n422:                     ACC contents = windowState.get();\n423:                     if (contents != null) {\n424:                         emitWindowContents(window, contents);\n425:                     }\n426:                 }\n427: \n428:                 if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n429:                     windowState.clear();\n430:                 }\n431:                 registerCleanupTimer(window);\n432:             }\n433:         }\n434: \n435:         // side output input event if\n436:         // element not handled by any window\n437:         // late arriving tag has been set\n438:         // windowAssigner is event time and current timestamp + allowed lateness no less than\n439:         // element timestamp\n440:         if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)) {\n441:             if (lateDataOutputTag != null) {\n442:                 sideOutput(element);\n443:             } else {\n444:                 this.numLateRecordsDropped.inc();\n445:             }\n446:         }\n447:     }\n448: \n449:     @Override\n450:     public void onEventTime(InternalTimer<K, W> timer) throws Exception {\n451:         triggerContext.key = timer.getKey();\n452:         triggerContext.window = timer.getNamespace();\n453: \n454:         MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows;\n455: \n456:         if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n457:             mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n458:             W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(triggerContext.window);\n459:             if (stateWindow == null) {\n460:                 // Timer firing for non-existent window, this can only happen if a\n461:                 // trigger did not clean up timers. We have already cleared the merging\n462:                 // window and therefore the Trigger state, however, so nothing to do.\n463:                 return;\n464:             } else {\n465:                 windowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n466:             }\n467:         } else {\n468:             windowState.setCurrentNamespace(triggerContext.window);\n469:             mergingWindows = null;\n470:         }\n471: \n472:         TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onEventTime(timer.getTimestamp());\n473: \n474:         if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n475:             ACC contents = windowState.get();\n476:             if (contents != null) {\n477:                 emitWindowContents(triggerContext.window, contents);\n478:             }\n479:         }\n480: (mergingWindows != null) {\n491:             // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n492:             mergingWindows.persist();\n493:         }\n494:     }\n495: \n496:     @Override\n497:     public void onProcessingTime(InternalTimer<K, W> timer) throws Exception {\n498:         triggerContext.key = timer.getKey();\n499:         triggerContext.window = timer.getNamespace();\n500: \n501:         MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows;\n502: \n503:         if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n504:             mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n505:             W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(triggerContext.window);\n506:             if (stateWindow == null) {\n507:                 // Timer firing for non-existent window, this can only happen if a\n508:                 // trigger did not clean up timers. We have already cleared the merging\n509:                 // window and therefore the Trigger state, however, so nothing to do.\n510:                 return;\n511:             } else {\n512:                 windowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n513:             }\n514:         } else {\n515:             windowState.setCurrentNamespace(triggerContext.window);\n516:             mergingWindows = null;\n517:         }\n518: \n519:         TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onProcessingTime(timer.getTimestamp());\n520: \n521:         if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n522:             ACC contents = windowState.get();\n523:             if (contents != null) {\n524:                 emitWindowContents(triggerContext.window, contents);\n525:             }\n526:         }\n527: \n528:         if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n529:             windowState.clear();\n530:         }\n531: \n532:         if (!windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n533:                 && isCleanupTime(triggerContext.window, timer.getTimestamp())) {\n534:             clearAllState(triggerContext.window, windowState, mergingWindows);\n535:         }\n536: \n537:         if (mergingWindows != null) {\n538:             // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n539:             mergingWindows.persist();\n540:         }\n541:     }\n542: \n543:     @Override\n544:     public OperatorAttributes getOperatorAttributes() {\n545:         boolean isOutputOnlyAfterEndOfStream =\n546:                 windowAssigner instanceof GlobalWindows\n547:                         && trigger instanceof GlobalWindows.EndOfStreamTrigger;\n548:         return new OperatorAttributesBuilder()\n549:                 .setOutputOnlyAfterEndOfStream(isOutputOnlyAfterEndOfStream)\n550:                 .build();\n551:     }\n552: \n553:     /**\n554:      * Drops all state for the given window and calls {@link Trigger#clear(Window,\n555:      * Trigger.TriggerContext)}.\n556:      *\n557:      * <p>The caller must ensure that the correct key is set in the state backend and the\n558:      * triggerContext object.\n559:      */\n560:     private void clearAllState(\n561:             W window, AppendingState<IN, ACC> windowState, MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows)\n562:             throws Exception {\n563:         windowState.clear();\n564:         triggerContext.clear();\n565:         processContext.window = window;\n566:         processContext.clear();\n567:         if (mergingWindows != null) {\n568:             mergingWindows.retireWindow(window);\n569:             mergingWindows.persist();\n570:         }\n571:     }\n572: \n573:     /** Emits the contents of the given window using the {@link InternalWindowFunction}. */\n574:     @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n575:     private void emitWindowContents(W window, ACC contents) throws Exception {\n576:         timestampedCollector.setAbsoluteTimestamp(window.maxTimestamp());\n577:         processContext.window = window;\n578:         userFunction.process(\n579:                 triggerContext.key, window, processContext, contents, timestampedCollector);\n580:     }\n581: \n582:     /**\n583:      * Write skipped late arriving element to SideOutput.\n584:      *\n585:      * @param element skipped late arriving element to side output\n586:      */\n587:     protected void sideOutput(StreamRecord<IN> element) {\n588:         output.collect(lateDataOutputTag, element);\n589:     }\n590: \n591:     /**\n592:      * Retrieves the {@link MergingWindowSet} for the currently active key. The caller must ensure\n593:      * that the correct key is set in the state backend.\n594:      *\n595:      * <p>The caller must also ensure to properly persist changes to state using {@link\n596:      * MergingWindowSet#persist()}.\n597:      */\n598:     protected MergingWindowSet<W> getMergingWindowSet() throws Exception {\n599:         @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n600:         MergingWindowAssigner<? super IN, W> mergingAssigner =\n601:                 (MergingWindowAssigner<? super IN, W>) windowAssigner;\n602:         return new MergingWindowSet<>(mergingAssigner, mergingSetsState);\n603:     }\n604: \n605:     /**\n606:      * Returns {@code true} if the watermark is after the end timestamp plus the allowed lateness of\n607:      * the given window.\n608:      */\n609:     protected boolean isWindowLate(W window) {\n610:         return (windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n611:                 && (cleanupTime(window) <= internalTimerService.currentWatermark()));\n612:     }\n613: \n614:     /**\n615:      * Decide if a record is currently late, based on current watermark and allowed lateness.\n616:      *\n617:      * @param element The element to check\n618:      * @return The element for which should be considered when sideoutputs\n619:      */\n620:     protected boolean isElementLate(StreamRecord<IN> element) Let me analyze the `processElement` method (lines 293-447) to understand the bug:\n\nLooking at the code:\n1. Lines 303-403: For merging windows (MergingWindowAssigner):\n   - It adds windows and performs merges\n   - **Line 369: `if (isWindowLate(actualWindow))` checks if the window is late**\n   - If the window is late, it retires the window and continues (skips to next window)\n   - This check happens AFTER the merge\n\n2. Lines 404-433: For non-merging windows:\n   - Lines 408-410: Checks if window is late and skips it\n\n3. Lines 440-446: Late element side output handling:\n   - **`if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element))`**\n   - This checks if the element was skipped by all windows AND if it's considered late\n   - If both conditions are true and `lateDataOutputTag` is not null, it calls `sideOutput(element)`\n   - Otherwise, it increments the dropped counter\n\nNow, the problem description states: \"An element arrives with a timestamp past the current watermark. The `WindowOperator.processElement()` method calls `isElementLate()` which returns true. For merging windows, this element could have been merged into an existing window that hasn't been cleaned up yet. The late element handling code path attempts `sideOutput()` but the output collector may not have the tag registered, causing the element to be silently dropped.\"\n\nBut looking at the code more carefully, I notice that:\n- `isWindowLate()` checks if `window.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness <= currentWatermark()`\n- `isElementLate()` checks if `element.getTimestamp() + allowedLateness <= currentWatermark()`\n\nThe bug is: For a late element that arrives after the watermark but could still extend a session window:\n\n\n- The element's timestamp plus allowedLateness might already be past the watermark (making `isElementLate()` return true)\n- Yet it could still merge into an existing session window that hasn't expired\n- When checking `isWindowLate(actualWindow)` at line 369, the window's end time plus allowedLateness might still be in the future (returning false)\n- So the element gets processed by the window rather than skipped, but since it's marked late, the side output logic at line 440 tries to emit it to a tag that may not be registered or could be null, causing the emission to fail silently\n\n. The real issue is in the logic flow: when a late element arrives after the watermark but before a merging window expires, it gets absorbed into that window and marked as processed. This prevents the late data check at line 440 from triggering since `isSkippedElement` is false. The element bypasses the side output entirely even though it technically arrived late. The element gets dropped as late data since the window end time plus allowed lateness is well before the current watermark. No windows get created or merged for this late arrival.\n\nHowever, there's a potential issue with merging windows. If an earlier element at 10000 creates window [10000, 13000), then a second element at 12000 extends it to [10000, 15000), the watermark advancing to 14600 doesn't immediately close this extended window since 15000 > 14600. When a third element arrives at 12500, it could merge into the still-open [10000, 15000) window. The question becomes whether this merging element gets flagged as late data based on its own timestamp relative to the watermark.\n\nIf the element at 12500 is considered late (12500 + 0 ≤ 14600), `isElementLate()` returns true. But once it merges with the existing window, `isSkippedElement` flips to false, preventing the side output from firing even though the element itself arrived after the watermark. @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    void testLateness(boolean enableAsyncState) throws Exception {\n        final int windowSize = 2;\n        final long lateness = 500;\n\n                  new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n lateness,\n                                lateOutputTag);\n\n        WindowOperatorBuilder<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> builder =\n                new WindowOperatorBuilder<>(\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1500));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1300));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(2300));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 2), 1999));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(2300));\n\n        // this will not be sideoutput because window.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness >\n        // currentWatermark\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1997));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(6000));\n\n        // this is 1 and not 3 because the trigger fires and purges\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1999));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(6000));\n\n        //   testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(7000));\n\n        lateExpected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1998));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(7000));\n\n Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"SideOutput was not correct.\",\n                lateExpected,\n                (Iterable) @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    void testCleanupTimeOverflow(boolean enableAsyncState) throws Exception {\n        final int windowSize = 1000;\n        final long lateness = 2000;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        TumblingEventTimeWindows windowAssigner =\n                TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofMillis(windowSize));\n\n        final WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                windowAssigner,\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new PassThroughWindowFunction<\n                                                String, TimeWindow, Tuple2<String, Integer>>()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                lateness,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        WindowOperatorBuilder<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> builder =\n                new WindowOperatorBuilder<>(\n                        windowAssigner,\n                        EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                        new ExecutionConfig(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE,\n                        new TupleKeySelector(),\n                        TypeInformation.of(String.class));\n        builder.allowedLateness(Duration.ofMillis(lateness));\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>>\n                testHarness =\n                        enableAsyncState\n                                ? createAsyncTestHarness(\n                                        builder.asyncReduce(\n                                                new SumReducer(),\n                                                new PassThroughWindowFunction<>()))\n                                : createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        long timestamp = Long.MAX_VALUE - 1750;\n        Collection<TimeWindow> windows =\n                windowAssigner.assignWindows(\n                        new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1),\n                        timestamp,\n                        new WindowAssigner.WindowAssignerContext() {\n                            @Override\n                            public long getCurrentProcessingTime() {\n                                return enableAsyncState\n                                        ? ((AsyncWindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>)\n                                                        testHarness.getOperator())\n                                                .getWindowAssignerContext()\n                                                .getCurrentProcessingTime()\n                                        : ((WindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>)\n                                                        testHarness.getOperator())\n                                                .windowAssignerContext.getCurrentProcessingTime();\n                            }\n                        });\n        TimeWindow window = Iterables.getOnlyElement(windows);\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), timestamp));\n\n        // the garbage collection timer would wrap-around\n        assertThat(window.maxTimestamp() + lateness).isLessThan(window.maxTimestamp());\n\n        // and it would prematurely fire with watermark (Long.MAX_VALUE - 1500)\n        assertThat(window.maxTimestamp() + lateness).isLessThan(Long.MAX_VALUE - 1500);\n\n        // if we don't correctly prevent wrap-around in the garbage collection\n        // timers this watermark will clean our window state for the just-added\n        // element/window\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(Long.MAX_VALUE - 1500));\n\n        // this watermark is before the end timestamp of our only window\n        assertThat(window.maxTimestamp()).isStrictlyBetween(Long.MAX_VALUE - 1500, Long.MAX_VALUE);\n\n        // push in a watermark that will trigger computation of our window\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(window.maxTimestamp()));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(Long.MAX_VALUE - 1500));\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), window.maxTimestamp()));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(window.maxTimestamp()));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expected,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name elements (2400)\n        // are assigned to windows ending at 2999, 3999, 4999.\n        // The 2999 is dropped because it is already late (WM = 2999) but the rest are kept.\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 2400));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 2400));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 3001));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 3900));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(6000));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 5), 3999));\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 3999));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 4), 4999));\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 4999));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 5999));\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 5999));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(6000));\n\n        // sideoutput element due to lateness\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 3001));\n        sideExpected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 3001));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(25000));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(25000));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expected,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"SideOutput was not correct.\",\n                sideExpected,\n                (Iterable) testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void testSideOutputDueToLatenessSessionZeroLatenessPurgingTrigger() throws Exception {\n        final int gapSize = 3;\n        final long lateness = 0;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(gapSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new ReducedSessionWindowFunction()),\n                                PurgingTrigger.of(EventTimeTrigger.create()),\n                                lateness,\n                                lateOutputTag);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> sideExpected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1999));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1999));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 2000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(4998));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(4998));\n\n        // this will not be dropped because the session we're adding two has maxTimestamp\n        // after the current watermark\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 4500));\n\n        // new session\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 8500));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(7400));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(7400));\n\n        // this will merge the two sessions into one\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 7000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(11501));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-5\", 1000L, 11500L), 11499));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(11501));\n\n        // new session\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 11600));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(14600));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-1\", 11600L, 14600L), 14599));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(14600));\n\n        // this is side output as late\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 10000));\n        sideExpected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 10000));\n\n        // this is also side output as late (we test that they are not accidentally merged)\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 10100));\n        sideExpected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 10100));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 14500));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(20000));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-1\", 14500L, 17500L), 17499));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(20000));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(100000));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(100000));\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>> sideActual =\n                testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"SideOutput was not correct.\",\n                sideExpected,\n                (Iterable) sideActual,\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void testSideOutputDueToLatenessSessionZeroLateness() throws Exception {\n     // same as testSideOutputDueToLatenessSessionWithLateness() but with an accumulating\n        // trigger, i.e.\n        // one that does not return FIRE_AND_PURGE when firing but just FIRE. The expected\n        // results are therefore slightly different.\n\n        final int gapSize = 3;\n        final long lateness = 10;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(gapSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new ReducedSessionWindowFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                lateness,\n                                lateOutputTag /* late data output tag */);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1999));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1999));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 2000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(4998));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(4998));\n\n        // this will not be sideoutput because the session we're adding two has maxTimestamp\n        // after the current watermark\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 4500));\n\n        // new session\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 8500));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(7400));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(7400));\n\n        // this will merge the two sessions into one\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 7000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(11501));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-5\", 1000L, 11500L), 11499));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(11501));\n\n        // new session\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 11600));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(14600));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-1\", 11600L, 14600L), 14599));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(14600));\n\n        // because of the small allowed lateness and because the trigger is accumulating\n        // this will be merged into the session (11600-14600) and therefore will not\n        // be sideoutput as late\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 10000));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 14500));\n\n        // adding (\"key2\", 1) extended the session to (10000-146000) for which\n        // maxTimestamp <= currentWatermark. Therefore, we immediately get a firing\n        // with the current version of EventTimeTrigger/EventTimeTriggerAccum\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-2\", 10000L, 14600L), 14599));\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>> sideActual =\n                testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        assertThat(sideActual).isNull();\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(20000));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-3\", 10000L, 17500L), 17499));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(20000));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(100000));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(100000));\n\n        actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        sideActual = testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        assertThat(sideActual).isNull();\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void expected.add(new Watermark(14600));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 10000));\n\n        // the maxTimestamp of the merged session is already late,\n        // so we get an immediate firing\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-7\", 1000L, 14600L), 14599));\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>> sideActual =\n                testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        assertThat(sideActual).isNull();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 14500));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(20000));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-8\", 1000L, 17500L), 17499));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(20000));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(100000));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(100000));\n\n        actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        sideActual = testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        assertThat(sideActual).isNull();\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name = ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    //  UDFs\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    private static class PassThroughFunction\n            implements WindowFunction<\n                    Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        @Override\n        public void apply(\n                String k,\n                TimeWindow window,\n                Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>> input,\n                Collector<Tuple2<String, Integer>> out)\n                throws Exception {\n            for (Tuple2<String, Integer> in : input) {\n                out.collect(in);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class EmptyReturnFunction\n            implements WindowFunction<\n                    Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        @Override\n        public void apply(\n                String k,\n                TimeWindow window,\n                Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>> input,\n                Collector<Tuple2<String, Integer>> out)\n                throws Exception {}\n    }\n\n    private static class SumReducer implements ReduceFunction<Tuple2<String, Integer>> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        @Override\n        public Tuple2<String, Integer> reduce(\n                Tuple2<String, Integer> value1, Tuple2<String, Integer> value2) throws Exception {\n            return new Tuple2<>(value2.f0, value1.f1 + value2.f1);\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class RichSumReducer<W extends Window>\n            extends RichWindowFunction<\n                    Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, W> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        private boolean openCalled = false;\n\n        @Override\n        public void open(OpenContext openContext) throws Exception {\n            super.open(openContext);\n            openCalled = true;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void close() throws Exception {\n            super.close();\n            closeCalled.incrementAndGet();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void apply(\n                String key,\n                W window,\n                Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>> input,\n                Collector<Tuple2<String, Integer>> out)\n                throws Exception {\n\n            assertThat(openCalled).as(\"Open was not called\").isTrue();\n\n            int sum = 0;\n\n            for (Tuple2<String, Integer> t : input) {\n                sum += t.f1;\n            }\n            out.collect(new Tuple2<>(key, sum));\n        }\n    }\n\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    private static class Tuple2ResultSortComparator implements Comparator<Object>, Serializable {\n        @Override\n        public int compare(Object o1, Object o2) {\n            if (o1 instanceof Watermark || o2 instanceof Watermark) {\n                return 0;\n            } else {\n                StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>> sr0 =\n                        (StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>) o1;\n                StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>> sr1 =\n                        (StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>) o2;\n                if (sr0.getTimestamp() != sr1.getTimestamp()) {\n                    return (int) (sr0.getTimestamp() - sr1.getTimestamp());\n                }\n                int comparison = sr0.getValue().f0.compareTo(sr1.getValue().f0);\n                if (comparison != 0) {\n                    return comparison;\n                } else {\n                    return sr0.getValue().f1 - sr1.getValue().f1;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    private static class Tuple3ResultSortComparator implements Comparator<Object>, Serializable {\n        @Override\n        public int compare(Object o1, Object o2) {\n            if (o1 instanceof Watermark || o2 instanceof Watermark) {\n                return 0;\n            } else {\n                StreamRecord<Tuple3<String, Long, Long>> sr0 =\n                        (StreamRecord<Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>) o1;\n                StreamRecord<Tuple3<String, Long, Long>> sr1 =\n                        (StreamRecord<Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>) o2;\n                if (sr0.getTimestamp() != sr1.getTimestamp()) {\n                    return (int) (sr0.getTimestamp() - sr1.getTimestamp());\n                }\n                int comparison = sr0.getValue().f0.compareTo(sr1.getValue().f0);\n                if (comparison != 0) {\n                    return comparison;\n                } else {\n                    comparison = (int) (sr0.getValue().f1 - sr1.getValue().f1);\n                    if (comparison != 0) {\n                        return comparison;\n                    }\n                    return (int) (sr0.getValue().f2 - sr1.getValue().f2);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class TupleKeySelector implements KeySelector<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String> {\n
